headline: Pimco and BofA Back $14B Oracle Michigan Data Center in Landmark Deal
summary: Pacific Investment Management is in talks with Bank of America to finance a ~$14 billion Oracle data center in Michigan, one of the largest single data center debt deals on record. This signals institutional capital markets are now treating hyperscale cloud infrastructure as a bankable asset class at unprecedented scale. For Oracle, securing this financing accelerates capacity buildout without diluting equity, strengthening its ability to compete for long-duration enterprise and government AI workloads.
oci_implication: This deal validates OCI's aggressive capacity expansion thesis and gives Oracle a significant runway to close the infrastructure gap with AWS and Azure in key U.S. regions. Karan's product roadmap should anticipate materially more Michigan-region capacity coming online, creating an opportunity to anchor new enterprise deals around dedicated, sovereign-adjacent cloud deployments in the Midwest.
